tree.name: Dyson Daniels
tree.narrative: **Dyson Daniels** is what the Hawks got back when they decided the **Dejounte Murray** experiment was over. Atlanta shipped Murray to New Orleans in July 2024, and Daniels — an elite perimeter defender and the #8 pick in 2022 — headlined the return. Murray himself had cost the Hawks a massive haul of picks and **Danilo Gallinari** from San Antonio, making Daniels essentially the final return on that entire investment.
tree.tradeDescription: Hawks traded Dejounte Murray to Pelicans for Dyson Daniels, E.J. Liddell, Larry Nance Jr., Cody Zeller, 2025 1st (LAL, became Drake Powell), and 2027 1st (least favorable).
tree.note: Australian guard, elite perimeter defender. #8 pick in 2022 draft (via LAL/AD trade pick).

assetsGivenUp.name: Dejounte Murray
assetsGivenUp.narrative: **Dejounte Murray** was the Hawks' big swing in the summer of 2022. Atlanta sent **Danilo Gallinari**, three first-round picks, swap rights, and two seconds to San Antonio to land the All-Star point guard. The Murray-Young backcourt never quite clicked, and two years later he was flipped to New Orleans for Daniels and picks.
assetsGivenUp.tradeDescription: Hawks traded Danilo Gallinari, 2023 1st (CHO, didn't convey), 2025 1st (ATL own, became Carter Bryant), 2026 1st swap rights, and 2027 1st (ATL own) to Spurs for Murray and Jock Landale.
assetsGivenUp.note: All-Star PG, drafted #29 by SAS in 2016. ATL gave up massive pick haul to acquire him.

assetsGivenUp.name: Danilo Gallinari
assetsGivenUp.narrative: Signed as a free agent in 2021. **Danilo Gallinari** was a veteran stretch four who became salary filler in the Murray blockbuster to San Antonio.
assetsGivenUp.note: Signed as free agent. Veteran stretch four, sent to SAS in Murray trade.

assetsGivenUp.name: 2025 1st Round Pick via ATL
assetsGivenUp.narrative: Atlanta's own 2025 first-round pick, shipped to San Antonio as part of the steep price for **Dejounte Murray**. The Spurs used it to draft **Carter Bryant**.
assetsGivenUp.note: Atlanta's own 2025 1st-round pick, sent to SAS in Murray trade.

assetsGivenUp.name: 2026 2nd Round Pick via ATL
assetsGivenUp.narrative: Originally a Charlotte 2023 first-rounder with protections that never conveyed, converting to Atlanta's own 2026 second. Sent to San Antonio in the Murray deal.
assetsGivenUp.note: Originally a 2023 CHO 1st (top-16 protected '23, top-14 '24) — never conveyed, converted to ATL's own 2026 2nd. SAS included in De'Aaron Fox 3-team trade to SAC.

assetsGivenUp.name: 2027 2nd Round Pick via ATL
assetsGivenUp.narrative: Another converted Charlotte pick that ended up as Atlanta's own 2027 second-rounder, included in the Murray trade to San Antonio.
assetsGivenUp.note: Originally a 2023 CHO 1st (top-16 protected '23, top-14 '24) — never conveyed, converted to ATL's own 2027 2nd. SAS included in De'Aaron Fox 3-team trade to SAC.

assetsGivenUp.name: 2026 1st Round Pick
assetsGivenUp.narrative: Swap rights given to San Antonio — if Atlanta's 2026 first lands better than the Spurs', San Antonio can swap. Part of the Murray acquisition cost.
assetsGivenUp.note: SAS has right to swap 2026 1st-round picks with ATL if Atlanta's pick is better.

assetsGivenUp.name: 2027 1st Round Pick via ATL
assetsGivenUp.narrative: Atlanta's own 2027 first-round pick, the crown jewel of the draft capital sent to San Antonio for **Dejounte Murray**.
assetsGivenUp.note: Atlanta's own 2027 1st-round pick, sent to SAS in Murray trade.
